comment: Per-chunk gzip-size ceilings in bytes. CI runs scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs after `vite build` and fails the build if any chunk exceeds its budget by >10% (the slop in `slopPercent`). Budgets are sized 5% above the current measured chunks so non-trivial growth is what triggers the alert, not noise from a dependency point-update. Update the numbers here when a deliberate feature lands that grows a chunk — the diff in this file is what reviewers see. Last re-pinned Session 119: React Compiler disabled after the perf-trace comparison vs. Session 108 showed mixed results (all-actions p95 −45% but edit-heavy p95 +89% AND +24 KB gz). Index chunk back to 86.8 KB gz (was 108.8 with Compiler). New ceilings: flow 110 KB (unchanged); index 92 KB (back to Session 115's tight ceiling). The `react` chunk holding React 19's runtime + react-dom/client lands at 55 KB gz — left unbudgeted as a known vendor cost. Session 135 / Perf #13: dropped the `icons` budget — `lucide-react` is no longer pinned to its own chunk (the manualChunk forced the whole catalogue, including lazy-only icons, onto the eager path). Icons now co-locate with their consuming chunks; eagerly-used glyphs land in index (which still has comfortable headroom — measured ~70 KB gz after the change), lazy-only ones in their lazy dialog chunks. No single icons chunk left to budget. Session 180 / E6: re-pinned index 92 -> 105 KB. The index chunk had drifted to ~99.8 KB gz over many sessions (eager domain + pattern content accumulating); E6's reader-mode coaching registry + components and the new cost-accounting CRT pattern (all eager) were the increment that crossed the old 10% slop ceiling. Re-pinned ~5% above the measured 99.8 KB per the convention above. Session 180 (cont.): lazy-loaded the pattern library out of the eager path — `services/testHook.ts` was statically importing `patternById`, dragging every pattern build file into `index` (PatternLibraryDialog already lazy-loads it; testHook is boot-imported from main.tsx). `loadPattern` now uses a dynamic import, so the registry drops to its lazy chunk and index fell to 81.8 KB gz. Re-pinned 105 -> 88 KB (~5% above measured) — back to a tight ceiling.
